name: Why isn't my New York law firm showing up when people ask ChatGPT for **employment lawyers**?
description: **AI favors peer-reviewed recognitions like Chambers over websites; audit your third-party signals now.**[1][3]

mainEntity.name: Why isn't my New York law firm showing up when people ask ChatGPT for **employment lawyers**?

acceptedAnswer.text: **AI favors peer-reviewed recognitions like Chambers over websites; audit your third-party signals now.**[1][3]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Your firm likely lacks third-party recognitions like Chambers or Super Lawyers that AI prioritizes over website content.** AI tools such as ChatGPT and Perplexity pull from peer-reviewed directories rather than firm websites directly.[1][3] Firms without consistent signals across trusted sources remain invisible in synthesized responses.[2]

**Key factors:**
- No independent validations (e.g., Best Lawyers, Chambers)
- Inconsistent public data patterns
- Websites rarely cited directly
- AI reflects existing industry hierarchies

**Audit your AI presence immediately, strengthen peer recognitions, and align website bios with PR claims.** Tailor profiles to specific practices for better parsing.[1][2] **Consult a legal marketing expert familiar with New York directories.**
